Records.authors: Duhr, Claude, Johansson, Henrik, Kälin, Gregor, Mogull, Gustav, Verbeek, Bram
Records.description: Triangle-pentagon topology for the two-loop four-gluon amplitude in $N=2$ SQCD. Pentagon subgraph carries three external legs and triangle subgraph carries one. Diagrams (d) and (e) share this topology with pure adjoint and fundamental hypermultiplet loop content respectively. Propagator denominators defined in Ref. [Johansson:2017bfl]. At the conformal point $N_f=2N_c$, diagrams (d) and (e) manifestly cancel from the integrand. Diagram (d) contributes only to $N=4$ SYM part; (e) contributes only to $S$ (the non-conformal part).
Two-loop massless planar double box, Section 5.2. Loop-by-loop Baikov on the inner loop; twisted-cohomology produces an $\varepsilon$-factorised basis equivalent to Weinzierl:2022eaz.

Prescriptive-unitarity 'hexa-box' integral for two-loop six-gluon planar massless QCD amplitudes, defined as the special case $C=\emptyset$ of the general hexa-box $B$ integrals of Table III of Bourjaily:2019gqu. In this special case the scalar denominator structure is a planar penta-triangle (pentagon glued to a triangle along one internal edge, seven massless propagators, six external gluons) with pentagonal numerator $n_{hb}=s_{j+1,\ldots,k-1}[[k,b,c,j]]$ (Eq. (eq:Ihb)). Called $I_{n,jk}^{hb}$ in the paper and referred to as a 'hexa-box'. Vanishes when $k-1=j+1$. Reduced via IBP to the Henn:2025xrc UT basis; contributes to the IR-finite part of the hard function through weight four.
Records.location: Section 5, Eq. (eq:Ihb); Fig. fig:hexaboxcut (cut diagram in Fig. 2)

Records.authors: Gambuti, Giulio, Kosower, David A., Novichkov, Pavel P., Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Massless two-loop beetle (ice cream cone) integral with power-like double-collinear and soft-collinear divergences. The first example where kinematic-dependent Gram identities enter the finiteness constraints, introducing Mandelstam-variable-dependent generators. The truncated ideal of locally finite numerators has 12 generators (6 at rank 3, 6 at rank 4). No evanescent integrals exist within UV-finite power counting for this topology.

Records.authors: Bargiela, Piotr, Frellesvig, Hjalte, Marzucca, Robin, Morales, Roger, Seefeld, Florian, Wilhelm, Matthias, Yang, Tong-Zhi
Records.description: $I^{**}_{4,1,2}$ two-loop topology with three internal paths of lengths a=4, b=1, c=2 (neither v_L nor v_R carries external legs); 4-point integral, planar (b=1). Geometry: 0 ISPs; algebraic leading singularity; dlog form (§3.4 zero-ISPs)

Names: two-loop massless planar double box with four off-shell external legs
